results.title: The Soviet Union: epic land
results.creator_credit: Arnold Michaelis, Milan Herzog, Encyclopaedia Britannica Films and Novosti Press Agency
results.brief_description: A survey of the Soviet Union revealing its immense size, diverse geographical features and great range of ethnic groups, from the Uzbeks in their agricultural setting to residents of the city of Moscow at their recreations. Songs and instrumental music native to the areas shown are heard throughout the film.

results.title: The Sorcerer's apprentice (K)
results.brief_description: This is the classic Grimm’s fairytale brought to life by the narration of Vincent Price. This colourful animation tells of Spellbinder the Sorcerer who hires Hans as his apprentice, believing that Hans can neither read nor write. Hans is at first an enthusiastic apprentice, but after learning of Spellbinder’s evil ways, plans to use what he has read in the Book of Magic to defeat his master.

results.title: The Son
results.creator_credit: Julian Biggs, Michael Spencer and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: A dramatic portrayal of a not infrequent rural problem: the tendency of farmers’ sons to leave the land in favour of other employment. The serene pastoral countryside of southern Ontario provides the story background. Howard, an only son, has grown to manhood under his father’s domination, working for a weekly pittance that offers no independence. His resentment finally threatens a complete break from the farm. A legal partnership agreement between father and son provides an amicable solution. (Award: Toronto.)

results.title: The Solar system. - 2nd ed.
results.creator_credit: Thomas Smith and Encyclopaedia Britannica Films
results.brief_description: Investigates the structure and composition of the solar system, describes the origin of the Sun and its planets and surveys the major planetary bodies in the solar system. Suitable for upper primary and lower secondary levels.

results.title: The Solar system (MAC & PC)
results.creator_credit: Ken J. Widdowson, Educational Media International and Kodak Education Program
results.brief_description: Adapted from a video produced by David Widdowson of Astrovisuals, which provide over 100 spectacular high quality images from NASA, CSIRO, the European Space Agency, and the United States Naval Observatory. The CD-ROM may be used either in the classroom or for private use at home and covers subjects such as evolution of the solar system, evidence of water, ice, volcanoes and atmosphere of the planets, exploring space and cosmic impacts.
